Rugby League · 13s

The Backbone

Founded by a current Japan Samurais international, built alongside seven teammates who represented Japan at the inaugural Asia Cup. In a country where Rugby League is still writing its history — we're the ones writing it.

1
Japan International Founder
Current Japan Samurais player. 24 years of rugby experience across both codes.
7
Asia Cup Representatives
Seven Edo Cranes players on the pitch when Japan claimed Silver at the inaugural Asia Cup, Philippines 2025.
🥈
Asia Cup Silver · 2025
Japan Rugby League made history. This club was part of it.
27
Years · Head Coach
Canberra Raiders U18 development squad. Queensland University representative. Three caps for the Japan Samurais. International experience on both sides of the coaching board.

7s

We're building something. Rugby Sevens is coming to Edo Cranes — and we need the right people to build it with us.

No Rugby League experience needed. If you're fast, competitive, and want to be part of something from the ground up — this is your invitation.

WINGS
TSURU
Run Club · Tokyo · 翼鶴
First session launching now

You don't need to play rugby to be part of Edo Cranes. Wings Tsuru is our community arm — open to everyone in Tokyo, regardless of sport, background, or experience. Come for the run, stay for the community.

🏃
Weekly Runs
Regular group runs around Tokyo. All paces, all levels, all welcome.
🤝
Everyone Welcome
Expats, locals, rugby players, non-rugby players. No barriers to entry.
🦢
The Crane Community
Part of the Edo Cranes family. Same values, open doors, different pace.
Wings Tsuru Run Club
Run with us.

We're just getting started. Message us on Instagram for details on sessions, meeting points, and how to get involved. Photos and full schedule coming soon after our first run.
